name: Kamsa
shortDescription: Kamsa was a tyrannical ruler of the Vrishni kingdom with its capital at Mathura, known for his cruelty and persecution of the Yadava clan. He is primarily known as the maternal uncle of Krishna, who ultimately killed him.
description: Kamsa was a Yadava prince and the tyrannical king of Mathura. He was the son of Ugrasena, but seized the throne by imprisoning his father. Kamsa's reign was characterized by oppression and cruelty, particularly towards the Yadava clan. He is best known as the maternal uncle of Krishna. A prophecy foretold that Kamsa would be killed by Devaki's eighth son (Krishna). To prevent this, Kamsa imprisoned Devaki and her husband Vasudeva, killing their first six children. The seventh child, Balarama, was transferred to Rohini's womb, and the eighth, Krishna, was secretly taken to Gokula and raised by Nanda and Yashoda. Upon learning of Krishna's existence, Kamsa sent various demons to kill him, all of whom were defeated. Eventually, Krishna returned to Mathura and killed Kamsa, freeing Ugrasena and restoring him to the throne. Kamsa represents tyranny, fear, and the abuse of power, while his death symbolizes the triumph of good over evil and the restoration of dharma.
